sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Sean, is a male who presents with a history of back pain, specifically lower left back pain, which began after a microdiscectomy surgery in November 2020.
He describes the pain as "horrible" and "awful," with symptoms including nerve pain down his leg.
He was prescribed gabapentin in high doses for this condition, which he found difficult to withdraw from.
He reports that stretching helps alleviate the pain, but bending over and physical activities make it worse.
He also mentions that the pain is not always present, but can occur randomly.
Past medical history: The patient had a microdiscectomy surgery in November 2020 for a disk break that wrapped around and connected to his spinal thecal sac.
Review of systems: The patient reports pain in his lower back, but does not mention any other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking gabapentin, and will be prescribed meloxicam and demerol.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient had no pain with flexion or extension, but reported pain when standing and when palpation was performed on the left side around the L5.
Dorsiflexion was normal, and the patient had normal range of motion.
There was no pain down his legs.
Test Results:
The patient had an x-ray, which showed no fractures or issues with the spinal bone, and the area where he had surgery had healed properly.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has a muscle pull due to a misalignment in his spine, confirmed by the physical exam and x-ray results.
The patient has left side low back pain; confirmed.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam for the patient's pain management, and referred him to pain management for further evaluation and discussion of alternative pain management options.
I also prescribed demerol.
The patient will start physical therapy to address the muscle pull and misalignment in his spine.
He will see a physical therapist at the VA.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me in [NOT MENTIONED].
